title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: Complete the text with ONE word only.

content.text: Biomimicry is the practice of looking to nature (0) inspiration to solve complex human problems. By studying the mechanics of a bird's wing or the water-repellent properties of a lotus leaf, engineers can develop technologies that are (9) more efficient than traditional models. One notable example is the Shinkansen bullet train in Japan, (10) redesigned nose was modeled after the beak of a kingfisher to reduce noise pollution. || (11) only does this approach lead to sustainable innovation, but it also highlights how much we have yet to learn from the natural world. (12) it not for the observation of sharkskin, for instance, hospitals might still be struggling to develop certain bacteria-resistant surfaces. (13) is widely recognized that nature has already solved many of the challenges we face today. However, (14) the vast potential of biomimicry, many industries have been slow to adopt these bio-inspired designs. This is often due to the high costs involved, (15) the long-term environmental benefits are clear. Ultimately, (16) is no doubt that as resources become scarcer, looking to nature will become a necessity rather than a choice.

questions.explanation: Adverb used as a modifier to emphasize a comparative adjective (more efficient).
Relative pronoun used to indicate possession, referring back to the 'Shinkansen bullet train'.
Negative inversion used with 'only' to start the correlative structure 'Not only... but also'.
Inversion in a second conditional structure used to express 'If it were not for'.
Dummy subject used in the passive reporting structure 'It is recognized that'.
Preposition used to introduce a contrast or concession, followed by a noun phrase.
Conjunction used to introduce a concessive clause.
Expletive pronoun used in the fixed expression 'There is no doubt that'.
